title: Cleft Sentences — News Analysis & Reporting
titleTr: Yarikli Cumleler — Haber Analizi ve Raporlama
description: Master cleft sentences to add focus and emphasis to your professional communication. Learn It-clefts, Wh-clefts, All-clefts, and reason/thing clefts — essential tools for analyzing news, writing reports, and leading strategy meetings. Eyüp analyzes a major supply chain disruption in a strategy meeting.
descriptionTr: Profesyonel iletisiminize odak ve vurgu katmak icin yarikli cumlelerde ustalasin. It-cleft, Wh-cleft, All-cleft ve reason/thing cleft'leri ogren — haberleri analiz etmek, rapor yazmak ve strateji toplantilari yonetmek icin temel araclar. Eyup bir strateji toplantisinda buyuk bir tedarik zinciri aksamisini analiz ediyor.

vocabulary.example: It is the lack of transparency that concerns us most about this supplier.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu tedarikci hakkinda bizi en cok endise ettiren seffaflik eksikligidir.
vocabulary.extra: It was the CEO who made the final decision, not the board.
vocabulary.extraTr: Nihai karari veren yonetim kurulu degil, CEO'ydu.

vocabulary.example: What we need is a complete overhaul of our supply chain strategy.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Ihtiyacimiz olan sey tedarik zinciri stratejimizin tamamen yenilenmesidir.
vocabulary.extra: What concerns me most is the speed at which this disruption is spreading.
vocabulary.extraTr: Beni en cok endise ettiren bu aksamanin yayilma hizidir.

vocabulary.example: The reason we lost market share is that we failed to diversify our suppliers.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Pazar payi kaybetmemizin nedeni tedarikcilarimi cesitlendirmede basarisiz olmamizdir.
vocabulary.extra: The reason this news is so alarming is that it affects the entire global network.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu haberin bu kadar endiselendirici olmasinin nedeni tum kuresel agi etkilemesidir.

vocabulary.turkish: mesele su ki / olay su ki
vocabulary.example: The thing that makes this crisis different is its unprecedented scale.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu krizi farkli kilan sey emsalsiz olcegi.
vocabulary.extra: The thing about supply chain disruptions is that they have a domino effect.
vocabulary.extraTr: Tedarik zinciri aksamalarindaki mesele domino etkisi yaratmalaridir.

vocabulary.example: All we need is a reliable backup supplier and a solid contingency plan.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tek ihtiyacimiz olan guvenilir bir yedek tedarikci ve saglam bir acil durum plani.
vocabulary.extra: All it takes is one disruption to expose weaknesses in the entire chain.
vocabulary.extraTr: Tum zincirdeki zayifliklari ortaya cikarmak icin tek gereken bir aksama.

vocabulary.example: The CEO placed special emphasis on supply chain resilience during the board meeting.
vocabulary.exampleTr: CEO yonetim kurulu toplantisinda tedarik zinciri dayanikliligina ozel vurgu yapti.
vocabulary.extra: Cleft sentences allow you to shift emphasis to the most important part of the message.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yarikli cumleler vurguyu mesajin en onemli kismina kaydirmaniza olanak tanir.

vocabulary.example: The report highlights three critical vulnerabilities in our distribution network.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Rapor dagitim agimizdaki uc kritik zayifligi vurguluyor.
vocabulary.extra: What I want to highlight is the correlation between cost-cutting and quality decline.
vocabulary.extraTr: Vurgulamak istedigim sey maliyet kesintisi ile kalite dususu arasindaki korelasyon.

vocabulary.example: Our immediate focus should be on securing alternative suppliers.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Acil odagimiz alternatif tedarikciler saglamak uzerine olmali.
vocabulary.extra: What we need to focus on is the root cause, not just the symptoms.
vocabulary.extraTr: Odaklanmamiz gereken sey sadece belirtiler degil, temel nedendir.

vocabulary.example: The underlying issue is not the disruption itself, but our lack of preparedness.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Altinda yatan sorun aksamanin kendisi degil, hazirliksiz olmamiz.
vocabulary.extra: We need to address the underlying causes, not just the surface-level problems.
vocabulary.extraTr: Sadece yuzeysel sorunlari degil, altinda yatan nedenleri ele almamiz gerekiyor.

vocabulary.example: There is a fundamental flaw in how we manage supplier relationships.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tedarikci iliskilerini yonetme seklimizde temel bir kusur var.
vocabulary.extra: What this crisis reveals is a fundamental weakness in global supply chains.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu krizin ortaya cikardigi sey kuresel tedarik zincirlerindeki temel bir zayifliktir.

vocabulary.example: The root cause of the disruption was over-reliance on a single supplier.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Aksamanin temel nedeni tek bir tedarikciye asiri bagimlilikti.
vocabulary.extra: It is the root cause that we must address, not the symptoms.
vocabulary.extraTr: Ele almamiz gereken belirtiler degil, temel nedendir.

vocabulary.example: There's a strong correlation between supplier diversification and supply chain resilience.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tedarikci cesitlendirmesi ile tedarik zinciri dayanikliligi arasinda guclu bir korelasyon var.
vocabulary.extra: What the data shows is a clear correlation between investment in technology and efficiency gains.
vocabulary.extraTr: Verilerin gosterdigi teknolojiye yatirim ile verimlilik kazanimlari arasinda acik bir korelasyondur.

vocabulary.example: The implications of this supply chain crisis extend far beyond the logistics sector.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu tedarik zinciri krizinin sonuclari lojistik sektorunun cok otesine uziyor.
vocabulary.extra: What concerns me is the long-term implications for our European operations.
vocabulary.extraTr: Beni endise ettiren Avrupa operasyonlarimiz icin uzun vadeli sonuclari.

vocabulary.example: The ramifications of this single decision could affect our operations for years.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu tek kararin zincirleme sonuclari operasyonlarimizi yillarca etkileyebilir.
vocabulary.extra: All it takes is one wrong move, and the ramifications are enormous.
vocabulary.extraTr: Tek gereken bir yanlis hamle ve zincirleme sonuclar muazzam.

vocabulary.example: The scale of this supply chain disruption is absolutely unprecedented.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu tedarik zinciri aksamisinin olcegi kesinlikle emsalsiz.
vocabulary.extra: It is the unprecedented nature of this crisis that makes planning so difficult.
vocabulary.extraTr: Planlamayi bu kadar zorlastiran bu krizin emsalsiz dogas.

vocabulary.example: There has been a significant increase in shipping costs over the past quarter.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Gecen ceyrekte nakliye maliyetlerinde kayda deger bir artis oldu.
vocabulary.extra: What is significant about this report is the data on emerging markets.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu raporda kayda deger olan gelismekte olan pazar verisidir.

vocabulary.example: We've seen a substantial decline in on-time deliveries since the disruption began.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Aksama basladigindan beri zamaninda teslimelarda hatiri sayilir bir dusus gorduk.
vocabulary.extra: The financial impact has been substantial — over $2 million in losses this quarter.
vocabulary.extraTr: Finansal etki buyuk oldu — bu ceyrek 2 milyon doların uzerinde kayip.

vocabulary.example: The improvement in delivery times was marginal — only 2% better than last quarter.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Teslimat surelerindeki iyilesme marjinaldi — gecen ceyrege gore sadece %2 daha iyi.
vocabulary.extra: All we achieved was a marginal cost reduction, which is not enough.
vocabulary.extraTr: Elde ettigimiz tek sey marjinal bir maliyet azaltmasiydi, bu yeterli degil.

vocabulary.example: The risk of a second disruption was initially considered negligible.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Ikinci bir aksama riski baslangicta ihmal edilebilir kabul edildi.
vocabulary.extra: The reason we ignored the warning signs is that the impact seemed negligible at the time.
vocabulary.extraTr: Uyari isretlerini gormezden gelmemizin nedeni o sirada etkinin ihmal edilebilir gorunmesiydi.

vocabulary.example: The disruption had a profound impact on global trade routes and shipping costs.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Aksama kuresel ticaret rotalari ve nakliye maliyetleri uzerinde derin bir etki yaratti.
vocabulary.extra: What makes this analysis noteworthy is the profound insight it offers into market behavior.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu analizi kayda deger kilan pazar davranisina sundugu derin icgorudur.

vocabulary.example: There has been a dramatic increase in demand for warehouse space since the crisis.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Krizden bu yana depo alani talebinde dramatik bir artis oldu.
vocabulary.extra: The thing that surprised everyone was the dramatic speed of the market reaction.
vocabulary.extraTr: Herkesi sasirtan sey piyasa tepkisinin dramatik hiziydi.

vocabulary.example: Recovery from the supply chain crisis will be gradual, not immediate.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tedarik zinciri krizinden toparlanma ani degil, kademeli olacak.
vocabulary.extra: What we're seeing is a gradual shift toward regional supply chains.
vocabulary.extraTr: Gordugumuz sey bolgesel tedarik zincirlerine dogru kademeli bir kaymadır.

vocabulary.example: Given the current trends, another supply chain disruption is almost inevitable.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Mevcut egilimler goz onune alindiginda, baska bir tedarik zinciri aksamasi neredeyse kacinilmaz.
vocabulary.extra: The reason restructuring is inevitable is that our current model is unsustainable.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yeniden yapilanmanin kacinilmaz olmasinin nedeni mevcut modelimizin surdurulemez olmasi.

vocabulary.example: The decision to move production offshore was highly controversial among stakeholders.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Uretimu denizasiri tasima karari paydaslar arasinda son derece tartismaliydi.
vocabulary.extra: What makes this report controversial is its claim that reshoring is more cost-effective.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu raporu tartismali yapan yurt icine geri donusun daha maliyet-etkin oldugu iddiasi.

vocabulary.example: The allocation of emergency resources became a contentious issue during the crisis.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Acil durum kaynaklarinin tahsisi kriz sirasinda cekismeli bir konu oldu.
vocabulary.extra: The most contentious point in the meeting was whether to cut costs or invest more.
vocabulary.extraTr: Toplantidaki en cekismeli nokta maliyetleri mi kesmek yoksa daha fazla mi yatirim yapmak olduydu.

vocabulary.example: The rate of supply chain failures this year is alarming.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu yilki tedarik zinciri basarisizlik orani endiselendirici.
vocabulary.extra: What is most alarming is the lack of contingency planning across the industry.
vocabulary.extraTr: En endise verici olan sektor genelinde acil durum planlama eksikligidir.

vocabulary.example: Despite the crisis, there are some promising developments in regional sourcing.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Krize ragmen, bolgesel kaynak kullaniminda bazi umut verici gelismeler var.
vocabulary.extra: What is promising about this approach is its potential to reduce dependency on single markets.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu yaklasimda umut verici olan tek pazarlara bagimililgi azaltma potansiyeli.

vocabulary.example: It is noteworthy that three major competitors have already shifted to regional supply chains.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Uc buyuk rakibin zaten bolgesel tedarik zincirlerine gecmis olmasi kayda deger.
vocabulary.extra: The most noteworthy finding in the report is the 40% cost increase prediction.
vocabulary.extraTr: Rapordaki en kayda deger bulgu yuzde 40'lik maliyet artisi tahminidir.

vocabulary.example: The speed at which companies adapted to the disruption was remarkable.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Sirketlerin aksamaya uyum saglama hizi dikkat cekiciydi.
vocabulary.extra: What is remarkable about this crisis is how it exposed hidden vulnerabilities.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu krizde dikkat cekici olan gizli zayifliklari nasil ortaya cikardigidir.

vocabulary.example: The most striking finding is the correlation between preparedness and recovery speed.
vocabulary.exampleTr: En carpici bulgu hazirlik ile toparlanma hizi arasindaki korelasyon.
vocabulary.extra: It is striking how quickly the market shifted after the disruption was announced.
vocabulary.extraTr: Aksama duyurulduktan sonra piyasanin ne kadar hizli degistigi carpici.

grammar.title: Cleft Sentences for Emphasis
grammar.explanation: Cleft sentences split a simple sentence into two parts to emphasize one particular element. Instead of saying 'We need better planning,' you can say 'What we need is better planning' — this puts FOCUS on 'better planning.' Cleft sentences are essential in news analysis, business reports, strategy meetings, and academic writing. They help you sound analytical, precise, and authoritative.
grammar.explanationTr: Yarikli cumleler basit bir cumleyi, belirli bir ogeyi vurgulamak icin iki parcaya boler. 'We need better planning' demek yerine 'What we need is better planning' diyebilirsiniz — bu ODAGI 'better planning' uzerine koyar. Yarikli cumleler haber analizinde, is raporlarinda, strateji toplantilarinda ve akademik yazida vazgecilmezdir. Analitik, kesin ve otoriter durmanizi saglar.
table: It-cleft: It is/was + focused element + that/who + rest of sentence
It is the lack of planning that caused this crisis.
It was our logistics team who identified the problem first.
Puts focus on the element after 'It is/was'. Use 'who' for people, 'that' for things.
Wh-cleft: What + subject + verb + is/was + focused element
What we need is a complete strategy overhaul.
What concerns me most is the speed of this disruption.
Starts with 'What' and puts the FOCUS at the END of the sentence. Very common in meetings.
All-cleft: All + subject + verb + is/was + focused element
All we need is a reliable backup supplier.
All it takes is one disruption to expose the weakness.
'All' minimizes — 'the only thing needed is...' Makes things sound simple and focused.
The reason-cleft: The reason + (why) + clause + is + that + clause
The reason we lost market share is that we didn't diversify.
The reason this is alarming is that it affects the entire network.
Explains WHY something happened. Very useful in root cause analysis and reports.
The thing-cleft: The thing + that + clause + is + focused element
The thing that makes this different is its unprecedented scale.
The thing about this crisis is that nobody predicted it.
Conversational but still professional. Good for meetings and discussions.
The person/place/time-cleft: The person who... / The place where... / The time when...
The person who should take responsibility is the supply chain director.
The place where the disruption started was the Suez Canal.
Extended clefts for people, places, and times. Very specific and precise.
Negative cleft: It is not X that..., but Y / What we don't need is...
It is not the technology that failed, but the human oversight.
What we don't need is another theoretical solution — we need action.
Negative clefts correct misconceptions or redirect focus. Powerful in debates and analysis.

tips.en: Use It-clefts to correct or clarify: 'It wasn't the software that failed — it was the process.' This is perfect for meetings when someone blames the wrong thing.
tips.tr: It-cleft'leri duzeltmek veya aciklamak icin kullanin: 'It wasn't the software that failed — it was the process.' Bu, biri yanlis seyi suclayinca toplantilar icin mukemmel.

tips.en: Wh-clefts are the most common in business. Start your analysis with: 'What the data shows is...' 'What we need to focus on is...' 'What concerns me is...' Practice these three openers.
tips.tr: Wh-cleft'ler iste en yaygin olanlardir. Analizinize soyle baslayin: 'What the data shows is...' 'What we need to focus on is...' 'What concerns me is...' Bu uc acilisi pratik yapin.

tips.en: All-clefts are great for simplifying: 'All we need is one meeting to resolve this.' They make complex situations feel manageable.
tips.tr: All-cleft'ler basitlestirmek icin harikad: 'All we need is one meeting to resolve this.' Karmasik durumlari yonetilebilir hissettirir.

tips.en: Chain different clefts in analysis: 'The reason this happened is that... What makes it worse is that... All we can do now is...' This creates a logical flow.
tips.tr: Analizde farkli cleft'leri zincirleyin: 'The reason this happened is that... What makes it worse is that... All we can do now is...' Bu mantiksal bir akis olusturur.

tips.en: In news analysis, use clefts to highlight key findings: 'What is most striking about this report is...' 'It is the unprecedented scale that makes this newsworthy.' Sound like a journalist!
tips.tr: Haber analizinde, temel bulguları vurgulamak icin cleft kullanin: 'What is most striking about this report is...' 'It is the unprecedented scale that makes this newsworthy.' Bir gazeteci gibi duyulun!

tips.en: Avoid overusing clefts — one or two per paragraph is enough. Too many clefts make your writing feel heavy. Use them strategically for maximum impact.
tips.tr: Cleft'leri asiri kullanmaktan kacinin — paragraf basina bir veya iki yeterli. Cok fazla cleft yazinizi agir hissettirir. Maksimum etki icin stratejik kullanin.

reading.text:
THE GREAT SUPPLY CHAIN CRISIS: A NEWS ANALYSIS

It is the worst disruption to hit global supply chains in decades. What began as a localized factory shutdown in Southeast Asia has spiraled into a crisis affecting every continent. The question is not whether the impact will be significant — it is how long the ramifications will last.

The root cause of this crisis is not a single event but a combination of factors. What made the situation unprecedented was the simultaneous collapse of three critical supply corridors: maritime shipping through the Strait of Malacca, air freight capacity out of East Asia, and rail connections across Central Europe. It was this triple convergence that turned a manageable disruption into a full-blown crisis.

The reason this matters for every industry — not just logistics — is that modern supply chains are deeply interconnected. All it takes is one broken link to create a domino effect across the entire network. The thing that companies are now realizing is that decades of cost optimization created hidden vulnerabilities. What they sacrificed for efficiency was resilience.

The implications are profound. Shipping costs have increased by 340% since the crisis began — a substantial and alarming figure. The most striking finding in the latest industry report is that 78% of companies had no contingency plan for a disruption of this scale. It is this lack of preparedness that has drawn the most criticism from analysts.

What is promising, however, is the speed of the industry's response. Several noteworthy initiatives have emerged: regional supply chain hubs, AI-powered disruption prediction systems, and collaborative shipping networks. The person leading the most remarkable of these efforts is Dr. Amara Osei, whose research on resilient logistics has become the new paradigm for the industry.

All we need now is the collective will to implement these changes. What the crisis has taught us is that the old model of global supply chains — built on the assumption that disruptions would be marginal and manageable — is fundamentally broken. The reason restructuring is inevitable is not controversial: it is simply a matter of survival.
reading.textTr:
BUYUK TEDARİK ZİNCİRİ KRİZİ: BİR HABER ANALİZİ

On yillar icerisinde kuresel tedarik zincirlerini vuran en kotu aksama bu. Guneydogu Asya'da yerel bir fabrika kapatmasiyla baslayan sey her kitayi etkileyen bir krize donustu. Soru etkinin onemli olup olmadigi degil — zincirleme sonuclarin ne kadar surecegi.

Bu krizin temel nedeni tek bir olay degil, faktorlerin bir birlesimidir. Durumu emsalsiz yapan sey uc kritik tedarik koridorunun esanli cokmesiydi: Malakka Bogazi uzerinden deniz tasimaciigi, Dogu Asya'dan hava kargo kapasitesi ve Orta Avrupa'daki demiryolu baglantilari. Yonetilebilir bir aksamayi tam bir krize donusturen bu uclu yakinlasma oldu.

Bunun sadece lojistik degil her sektor icin onemli olmasinin nedeni modern tedarik zincirlerinin derinden birbirine bagli olmasıdır. Tum ag boyunca domino etkisi yaratmak icin tek gereken bir kirik halka. Sirketlerin simdi fark ettigi sey, onlarca yillik maliyet optimizasyonunun gizli zayifliklar yarattigidir. Verimlilik icin feda ettikleri sey dayaniklilikti.

Sonuclar derin. Kriz basladigindan bu yana nakliye maliyetleri %340 artti — hatiri sayilir ve endiselendirici bir rakam. En son sektor raporundaki en carpici bulgu sirketlerin %78'inin bu olcekte bir aksama icin acil durum planinin olmamasıdır. Analistlerden en cok elestiriyi ceken bu hazirliksizlik oldu.

Ancak umut verici olan sektorun tepki hizidir. Kayda deger bircok girisim ortaya cikti: bolgesel tedarik zinciri merkezleri, yapay zeka destekli aksama tahmin sistemleri ve isbirlikci nakliye aglari. Bu cabalarin en dikkat cekicisini yoneten kisi, dayanikli lojistik uzerine arastirmalari sektorde yeni paradigma haline gelen Dr. Amara Osei'dir.

Simdi tek ihtiyacimiz olan bu degisiklikleri uygulamak icin kolektif irade. Krizin bize ogrettigi sey, aksamalarin marjinal ve yonetilebilir olacagi varsayimi uzerine kurulu eski kuresel tedarik zinciri modelinin temelden bozuk oldugudir. Yeniden yapilanmanin kacinilmaz olmasinin nedeni tartismali degil: basitce hayatta kalma meselesi.

questions.question: What cleft structure is used in 'It is this lack of preparedness that has drawn the most criticism'?
questions.questionTr: 'It is this lack of preparedness that has drawn the most criticism' cumlesinde hangi cleft yapisi kullanilmistir?

questions.question: According to the text, what percentage of companies had no contingency plan?
questions.questionTr: Metne gore, sirketlerin yuzde kaci acil durum planina sahip degildi?

questions.question: What three supply corridors collapsed simultaneously?
questions.questionTr: Hangi uc tedarik koridoru esanli coktu?
options: Maritime through Malacca, air freight from East Asia, rail across Central Europe
Atlantic shipping, Pacific shipping, Arctic route

questions.question: What did companies sacrifice for efficiency according to the analysis?
questions.questionTr: Analize gore sirketler verimlilik icin neyi feda etti?

questions.question: Who is described as leading the most remarkable response effort?
questions.questionTr: En dikkat cekici mudahale cabasini yoneten kisi olarak kim tanimlaniyor?

dialog.title: Strategy Meeting: Analyzing the Supply Chain Crisis
dialog.titleTr: Strateji Toplantisi: Tedarik Zinciri Krizini Analiz Etme
dialog.context: Eyup, NovaTech'te bir strateji toplantisinda ekibiyle birlikte buyuk bir tedarik zinciri krizini analiz ediyor. Katilimcilar: Eyup (strateji muduru), Diana (tedarik zinciri muduru), Kerem (veri analisti), Selin (operasyon muduru), ve Mr. Harrison (ust yonetici).

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good morning, everyone. I've called this emergency strategy meeting because of the industry news that broke last night. Diana, can you summarize what happened?
lines.textTr: Gunaydin millet. Dun gece cikan sektor haberleri nedeniyle bu acil strateji toplantisini topladim. Diana, ne oldugunu ozetleyebilir misin?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Certainly. What we're looking at is a major supply chain disruption affecting three critical corridors simultaneously. The Strait of Malacca is partially blocked, East Asian air freight capacity has dropped by 60%, and rail connections across Central Europe are severely delayed.
lines.textTr: Tabii. Baktigimiz sey uc kritik koridoru esanli olarak etkileyen buyuk bir tedarik zinciri aksamasidir. Malakka Bogazi kismen kapali, Dogu Asya hava kargo kapasitesi %60 dustu ve Orta Avrupa demiryolu baglantilari ciddi sekilde gecikmeli.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thank you, Diana. What concerns me most about this situation is not the disruption itself — it is the unprecedented scale of it. The thing that makes this different from previous crises is that all three corridors are affected at the same time. It was the triple convergence that nobody predicted.
lines.textTr: Tesekkurler Diana. Bu durumda beni en cok endise ettiren aksamanin kendisi degil — emsalsiz olcegi. Bunu onceki krizlerden farkli kilan sey her uc koridorun ayni anda etkilenmesi. Kimsenin ongormedigi uclu yakinlasma oldu.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I've been analyzing the data overnight. What the numbers show is a dramatic increase in shipping costs — up 340% in just two weeks. The correlation between this disruption and commodity prices is striking. It is the food and electronics sectors that are being hit the hardest.
lines.textTr: Verileri gece boyunca analiz ediyordum. Rakamlarin gosterdigi nakliye maliyetlerinde dramatik bir artis — sadece iki haftada %340 yukseldi. Bu aksama ile emtia fiyatlari arasindaki korelasyon carpici. En sert vurulan gida ve elektronik sektorleri.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Those numbers are alarming. The reason we need to act immediately is that we have approximately $8 million in goods currently in transit through affected corridors. What we need right now is a clear picture of which shipments are at risk and which alternatives exist.
lines.textTr: Bu rakamlar endiselendirici. Derhal harekete gecmemizin nedeni su anda etkilenen koridorlardan transit geciste yaklasik 8 milyon dolarlik malimiz olmasi. Su anda ihtiyacimiz olan hangi sevkiyatlarin risk altinda oldugu ve hangi alternatiflerin mevcut oldugunun net bir resmi.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I've already mapped our exposure. The thing that worries me is that 65% of our Q2 inventory is on ships currently rerouting around the Strait of Malacca. All it takes is another two-week delay and we miss our commitments to three major clients.
lines.textTr: Maruziyetimizi zaten haritaladim. Beni endise ettiren sey su anda Malakka Bogazi etrafinda rota degistiren gemilerdeki Q2 envanterimizin %65'i. Tek gereken iki haftalik bir gecikme daha ve uc buyuk musteriye taahhutlerimizi kaciririz.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Understood. Here's what I propose. What we should focus on first is securing alternative air freight for the most time-sensitive shipments. It is the high-value electronics that we need to prioritize — those represent 40% of our Q2 revenue.
lines.textTr: Anlasildi. Onerim su. Once odaklanmamiz gereken en zaman duyarli sevkiyatlar icin alternatif hava kargo saglamak. Onceliklendirmemiz gereken yuksek degerli elektronikler — bunlar Q2 gelirimizin %40'ini temsil ediyor.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good. What about the underlying risk here? The reason I'm concerned long-term is that this could happen again. What guarantee do we have that our supply chain is resilient enough?
lines.textTr: Iyi. Peki buradaki altinda yatan risk? Uzun vadede endiselenmemin nedeni bunun tekrar olabilecegi. Tedarik zincirimizin yeterince dayanikli olduguna dair ne garantimiz var?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: That's the fundamental question, Mr. Harrison. The honest answer is: we don't have that guarantee today. The root cause of our vulnerability is over-reliance on three major shipping routes. What this crisis is teaching us is that we need a fundamentally different approach to supply chain design.
lines.textTr: Bu temel soru Bay Harrison. Durüst cevap su: bugun o garantimiz yok. Zayifligimizin temel nedeni uc buyuk nakliye rotasina asiri bagimlilik. Bu krizin bize ogrettigi temelden farkli bir tedarik zinciri tasarimi yaklasimine ihtiyacimiz oldugu.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I agree. The implication of staying with our current model is clear — we'll face the same crisis again. The thing about global supply chains is that they're only as strong as their weakest link.
lines.textTr: Katiliyorum. Mevcut modelimizle kalmanin sonucu acik — ayni krizle tekrar yuzlesecegiz. Kuresel tedarik zincirlerinin meselesi en zayif halkalari kadar guclu olmalari.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Exactly. So here is my strategic recommendation. What I'm proposing is a three-phase restructuring plan. Phase one: immediate crisis response — secure alternative routes for current shipments. Phase two: build regional supply chain hubs in Turkey, Mexico, and Poland within 12 months. Phase three: implement AI-powered disruption prediction across all operations.
lines.textTr: Aynen. Iste stratejik onerim. Onerim uc asamali bir yeniden yapilanma plani. Birinci asama: acil kriz mudahalesi — mevcut sevkiyatlar icin alternatif rotalar saglamak. Ikinci asama: 12 ay icinde Turkiye, Meksika ve Polonya'da bolgesel tedarik zinciri merkezleri kurmak. Ucuncu asama: tum operasyonlarda yapay zeka destekli aksama tahmini uygulamak.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: The data supports this. What my analysis shows is that companies with regional hubs recovered 3.5 times faster from the last disruption. It is the diversification factor that makes the biggest difference — not the technology alone.
lines.textTr: Veriler bunu destekliyor. Analizimin gosterdigi bolgesel merkezlere sahip sirketlerin son aksamadan 3.5 kat daha hizli toparlandigi. En buyuk farki yaratan cesitlendirme faktoru — tek basina teknoloji degil.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: What about the cost? Restructuring at this scale is not going to be cheap. The reason boards hesitate is that the ROI isn't immediately visible.
lines.textTr: Peki maliyet? Bu olcekte yeniden yapilanma ucuz olmayacak. Yonetim kurullarinin tereddut etmesinin nedeni yatirim getirisinin hemen gorunur olmamasi.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: You're right, the investment is substantial. But the reason this is not optional is that the cost of inaction is far greater. All we need to look at is this quarter's losses — $8 million in at-risk goods, potential contract penalties, and reputation damage. It is the cost of NOT restructuring that should alarm us, not the cost of restructuring.
lines.textTr: Haklisiniz, yatirim buyuk. Ama bunun istege bagli olmamasinin nedeni eylemsizligin maliyetinin cok daha buyuk olmasidir. Bakmamiz gereken tek sey bu ceyregin kayiplari — 8 milyon dolarlik risk altindaki mallar, potansiyel sozlesme cezalari ve itibar hasari. Bizi endiselendirmesi gereken yeniden yapilanmanin maliyeti degil, yeniden yapilanMAMANIN maliyeti.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I'd like to add something. What is promising about the regional hub model is that Turkey is perfectly positioned as a bridge between Europe and Asia. The ramifications of establishing a hub in Istanbul could be transformative for our entire European operation.
lines.textTr: Bir sey eklemek istiyorum. Bolgesel merkez modelinde umut verici olan Turkiye'nin Avrupa ve Asya arasinda mukemmel konumlanmis olmasi. Istanbul'da bir merkez kurmanin zincirleme sonuclari tum Avrupa operasyonumuz icin donusturucu olabilir.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Excellent point, Selin. The reason I specifically chose Turkey as one of our three hubs is that it offers access to both European and Middle Eastern markets. What makes Istanbul remarkable as a logistics hub is its dual-continent position and existing infrastructure.
lines.textTr: Mukemmel nokta, Selin. Uc merkezimizden biri olarak ozellikle Turkiye'yi secmemin nedeni hem Avrupa hem de Orta Dogu pazarlarina erisim saglamasi. Istanbul'u bir lojistik merkez olarak dikkat cekici yapan cift kitali konumu ve mevcut altyapisi.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Alright, I'm convinced. The thing that persuades me is the data Kerem shared — 3.5 times faster recovery is not a marginal improvement, it's a fundamental one. Eyüp, I want a detailed proposal on my desk by Friday. All I need is the budget breakdown and a realistic timeline.
lines.textTr: Tamam, ikna oldum. Beni ikna eden sey Kerem'in paylastigi veri — 3.5 kat daha hizli toparlanma marjinal bir iyilesme degil, temel bir iyilesme. Eyup, Cuma'ya kadar masamda detayli bir oneri istiyorum. Tek ihtiyacim olan butce dokumu ve gercekci bir zaman cizelgesi.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: You'll have it by Thursday, Mr. Harrison. What I want everyone in this room to understand is this: this crisis is not just a threat — it is an opportunity. It is the companies that act decisively now that will lead the industry in five years. The reason I'm confident about this plan is that it's built on data, not assumptions. Let's turn this crisis into our competitive advantage.
lines.textTr: Perşembe'ye kadar elinizde olacak Bay Harrison. Bu odadaki herkesin anlamsini istedigim su: bu kriz sadece bir tehdit degil — bir firsattir. Bes yil icinde sektore liderlik edecek olan simdi kararli davranan sirketlerdir. Bu plan hakkinda emin olmaimin nedeni varsayimlara degil, verilere dayanmasi. Bu krizi rekabet avantajimiza donusturelim.

questions.question: What is the root cause of NovaTech's vulnerability according to Eyüp?
questions.questionTr: Eyup'a gore NovaTech'in zayifliginin temel nedeni nedir?
options: Over-reliance on three major shipping routes

questions.question: What cleft structure does Eyüp use in 'What we need right now is a clear picture'?
questions.questionTr: Eyup 'What we need right now is a clear picture' cumlesinde hangi cleft yapisini kullaniyor?

questions.question: How many phases does Eyüp's restructuring plan have?
questions.questionTr: Eyup'un yeniden yapilanma plani kac asamadan olusuyor?

questions.question: Why did Eyüp specifically choose Turkey as a hub location?
questions.questionTr: Eyup neden ozellikle Turkiye'yi bir merkez lokasyonu olarak secti?
options: Access to both European and Middle Eastern markets

questions.question: What data point convinced Mr. Harrison to approve the plan?
questions.questionTr: Hangi veri noktasi Bay Harrison'i plani onaylamaya ikna etti?
options: 3.5 times faster recovery with regional hubs

fillBlanks.sentence: ___ is the lack of planning that caused this crisis.
What we ___ is a complete overhaul of our supply chain strategy.
The ___ we lost market share is that we didn't diversify our suppliers.
All it ___ is one disruption to expose the weakness.
The ___ that makes this crisis different is its unprecedented scale.
It was the CEO ___ made the final decision, not the board.
What ___ me most is the speed of this disruption.
The root ___ of the disruption was over-reliance on a single supplier.
The ___ of this decision could affect operations for years.
The scale of this disruption is absolutely ___.
There's a strong ___ between supplier diversification and resilience.
The improvement was ___ — only 2% better than last quarter.
The most ___ finding is the 340% cost increase.
Recovery from the crisis will be ___, not immediate.
Given current trends, another disruption is almost ___.

multipleChoice.question: "___ is the lack of transparency that concerns us most." Dogru baslangic?
"What we need ___ a complete strategy overhaul." Dogru fiil?
"The reason we failed ___ that we didn't prepare." Dogru baglac?
"All ___ takes is one mistake to ruin everything." Dogru zamir?
"It was the logistics team ___ identified the problem." Dogru ilgi zamiri?
"___ concerns me most is the speed of the disruption." Dogru soru kelimesi?
"The thing ___ makes this different is its scale." Dogru ilgi zamiri?
"It is not the technology that failed — ___ was the process." Dogru zamir?
Hangisi 'temel neden / kok neden' anlamina gelir?
"The ___ of this single decision are enormous." Dogru isim?
"The data reveals a ___ between investment and efficiency." Dogru isim?
"The decision was highly ___ among stakeholders." Dogru sifat?

sentenceOrder.correct: It is the lack of planning that caused this crisis
What we need is a complete strategy overhaul
The reason we failed is that we didn't prepare
All it takes is one disruption to expose the weakness
The thing that makes this different is its scale
It was the CEO who made the final decision
What concerns me most is the speed of this disruption
The implications of this crisis are profound
The root cause was over-reliance on a single supplier
Recovery from the crisis will be gradual not immediate

translation.turkish: Bizi en cok endise ettiren seffaflik eksikligi.
translation.english: It is the lack of transparency that concerns us most.
acceptAlso: What concerns us most is the lack of transparency

translation.turkish: Ihtiyacimiz olan tedarik zinciri stratejimizin tamamen yenilenmesi.
translation.english: What we need is a complete overhaul of our supply chain strategy.
acceptAlso: What we need is a complete supply chain strategy overhaul

translation.turkish: Pazar payi kaybetmemizin nedeni tedarikcileri cesitlendirmememiz.
translation.english: The reason we lost market share is that we didn't diversify our suppliers.
acceptAlso: The reason we lost market share is that we failed to diversify suppliers

translation.turkish: Tum zincirdeki zayifliklari ortaya cikarmak icin tek gereken bir aksama.
translation.english: All it takes is one disruption to expose weaknesses in the entire chain.
acceptAlso: All it takes is a single disruption to expose the weakness

translation.turkish: Bu krizi farkli yapan sey emsalsiz olcegi.
translation.english: The thing that makes this crisis different is its unprecedented scale.
acceptAlso: What makes this crisis different is its unprecedented scale

translation.turkish: Nihai karari veren CEO'ydu, yonetim kurulu degil.
translation.english: It was the CEO who made the final decision, not the board.
acceptAlso: It was the CEO, not the board, who made the final decision

translation.turkish: Beni en cok endise ettiren bu aksamanin hizi.
translation.english: What concerns me most is the speed of this disruption.
acceptAlso: The thing that concerns me most is the speed of this disruption

translation.english: The implications of this crisis are profound.
acceptAlso: The ramifications of this crisis are profound

translation.turkish: Temel neden tek bir tedarikciye asiri bagimlilikti.
translation.english: The root cause was over-reliance on a single supplier.
acceptAlso: The root cause of the disruption was over-reliance on a single supplier

translation.turkish: Krizden toparlanma ani degil, kademeli olacak.
translation.english: Recovery from the crisis will be gradual, not immediate.
acceptAlso: The recovery from this crisis will be gradual, not immediate
